commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

commit.message:
Fix video bg: always apply mix-blend-mode screen

Remove webm detection (unreliable on newer Safari). Apply screen blend
mode unconditionally - makes black pixels invisible on MP4 fallback,
no visual change on webm with native alpha transparency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add SVC as third diverse estimator in ensemble

SVC (RBF kernel) maximizes margin between classes — fundamentally
different from both LightGBM and ExtraTrees, giving the ensemble
genuine diversity across three different algorithm families.

- tune_svc(): Optuna HPO for C and gamma (log-scale), probability=True
  for soft voting; ML_OPTUNA_TRIALS_SVC=20 default (Platt scaling is slow)
- train.py: add SVC to VotingClassifier alongside LGBM + ExtraTrees
- settings.py: add optuna_trials_svc (ML_OPTUNA_TRIALS_SVC)
- .env.example: add ML_OPTUNA_TRIALS_SVC=20

  (belltown-cc.org), parses meeting dates from post titles, extracts

  meeting entry in recurring.yaml.

- Add radix to parseInt calls to prevent unexpected octal parsing
- Validate JSON response shape before accessing .posts property
- Guard array[0] accesses in tests with length assertions

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YX3ynwSkZaPbRYKTEwQX5y

fix: transform content type text→input_text for Responses API

Chat Completions uses "type": "text" in message content blocks, but the

provider rejects requests with "Invalid value: 'text'".

commit.message:
fix: address 20+ security, bug, and quality issues

Security:
- extractFlag: escape regex metacharacters, add word boundaries, support quoted values and hyphen-prefixed values
- Default DB path moved from /tmp to ~/.draft-warden/
- Remove unsafe printf JSON fallback in shim (require python3 or jq)
- Reject DRAFT_WARDEN_SECRET=changeme on startup
- Add CSP header to UI response

Bugs:
- Remove set -e from shim to fix swallowed error messages
- Add CHECK constraint for draft status in SQLite schema
- Fix race condition with atomic claimDraft() (UPDATE WHERE status=)
- Roll back status to pending on send failure
- Add ?status= query param to GET /api/drafts

Quality:
- Cache prepared statements via WeakMap
- Replace inline onclick with event delegation
- Escape single quotes in esc() function
- Add graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown
- Export extractFlag for direct testing
- Add test and test:watch scripts to package.json
- Fix rawArgs comment (was "JSON-stringified", is space-joined)
- Enable WAL journal mode for SQLite

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[fix] code prompt: embed full ATR stop-loss + OR-entry template to fix time_in_market=0 and MaxDD

Two hard rejections were 59% of all failures (time_in_market=0: 20%, MaxDD: 39%).
Root cause: template showed placeholder <exit_condition> and text warnings only.

Fix: replace generic template with complete working example that:
1. Shows ATR stop-loss + take-profit hardcoded into the for-loop skeleton
   (LLM copies the template → stop-loss is always present)
2. Shows OR-based entry pattern with comments explaining why
   (LLM learns: regime_ok AND (signal_A OR signal_B) fires on ~15% of days)
3. Adds ADAPTATION GUIDE explaining which lines to change vs keep
4. Collapses old scattered warnings into 2 clear rejection callouts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: TQQQstrategy
repository.description: AI strategy evolution

commit.message:
Balance: normalize weapon DPS, rebalance enemies, movement, XP

- All base weapons normalized to ~50 DPS at L1 with archetype curves
  (early-peak, linear, late-bloom)
- Evolution weapons buffed/nerfed to exceed parent L8 DPS at evo L1
- Nova Bolt nerfed from ~8050 to ~1050 DPS at L1
- Enemy base HP +30% across all types
- Enemy HP scaling coefficient 0.185 → 0.26
- XP drops: flat (no level scaling), subtle time-elapsed scaling (+1% per 10s)
- XP threshold: 240 → 160 (faster leveling)
- Movement speed -20% for all characters
- Surge: 5× density for 14s with progressive late-run scaling
- Magic Bolt L1 attack rate halved (cooldown 0.504 → 1.008s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add comprehensive test suite (82 tests)

- db.test.ts: CRUD, CHECK constraint, claimDraft, edge cases
- server.test.ts: all API routes, HMAC auth, extractFlag
- notify.test.ts: notification formatting and callbacks
- shim.test.ts: pass-through, interception, error propagation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.2: Reliability, security, and UX improvements

- Add AES-256-GCM encryption for API keys at rest with PBKDF2 key derivation and auto-migration
- Add custom provider support (any OpenAI-compatible API: Groq, Together, Fireworks, etc.)
- Add native filesystem browser with external drive support (/Volumes/) in Add Agent modal
- Add rich activity feed with expandable rows showing code diffs, tool results, shell output, errors
- Add CLI Monitor mode with real-time JSONL session mirroring from ~/.claude/projects/
- Add two-way chat with agents via --resume flag
- Add OpenAI OAuth (PKCE) provider
- Add broadcast throttling (250ms debounce per agent for agent:update events)
- Add CLI monitor hardening: debounced fs.watch, stale watcher pruning, tool_result parsing
- Add self-restart endpoint (POST /api/restart) with UI buttons and polling timeout
- Add reconnect sync (re-request agent list on Socket.io reconnect)
- Add project documentation: CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECT.md, BACKLOG.md, HANDOFF.md
- Fix stdin pipe blocking (stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"]) — root cause of agents stuck at "Launching..."
- Fix schedule interval assignment for proper cleanup on shutdown
- Update README with comprehensive feature list, security section, and changelog

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Comprehensive QA fixes across all games

CSS/Layout (screen filling):
- Fix #app missing height:100vh
- Correct game-layout height calc to account for top bar (38px) + padding
- Canvas height calc(100vh - 50px) properly fills viewport
- Game screen padding-top 38px matches actual top bar height
- Remove duplicate .game-label and .hs-tab CSS rules
- Fix returnToMenu() canvas height 360→400

Game Controller (game.js):
- Clear keys object on game start and return to menu (prevents stale input)
- Reset twoPlayer flag at start of every game (prevents score corruption)
- Skip exit modal if game is already over — go straight to menu
- Fix stage clear overlay: remove inline display:none that blocked CSS .visible

Audio (audio.js):
- Stop and clean up old oscillator nodes before each music loop restart
- Prevents 50ms overlap/clipping and memory leak from unbounded node array

Tetris:
- Soft drop speed uses min(2, gravity) so it never slows below natural gravity
- Fix wall size calculation — use consistent ratio-based approach

Mario 3:
- Fix world map navigation: allow moving to first level after start node
- Fix player palette: use correct property names (outfit/outfitDark/eyes/shirt)
- Fix player fireballs: isPlayerFireball flag prevents self-damage
- Player fireballs now damage enemies on contact
- Fireball/hammer projectiles properly created with gravity for hammers
- Reset _bossActivated/_bossDefeated flags on level start
- Slopes (16/17) and bridges (19) now solid — player doesn't fall through

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate all race conditions, update documentation

- StatsController.refresh() now routes through FeedScheduler.runFetchCycle()
  instead of spawning unguarded threads (root cause of $5 API cost overrun)
- FeedScheduler.runFetchCycle() made public, serves as single entry point
  for all fetch+process paths (scheduler, startup, manual refresh)
- AiSummaryService re-fetches article from DB before each Claude API call
  to verify it hasn't been processed by another thread
- CLAUDE.md: added "Concurrency Safety" section documenting the guards
  and warning against bypassing FeedScheduler
- README.md: comprehensive rewrite with architecture diagram, data flow,
  cost optimization section, all 7 RSS sources, full config reference

commit.message:
fix(ci): use pnpm exec tsx for database seed to resolve tsx not found

pnpm --filter db:seed fails because tsx binary isn't in PATH when pnpm
runs a nested package script without local node_modules. Using
'cd packages/database && pnpm exec tsx' directly finds tsx from
root node_modules/.bin via pnpm exec resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix iteratorsyncmers2 to produce identical output to MSP reference

iteratorsyncmers2 (AVX2-optimized) was producing ~34 superkmers where
the reference iteratorsyncmersmsp produced 16. Three root causes:

1. Bit encoding mismatch: get_storage_window read LSB-first but
   pack_32_bases stores MSB-first. Replaced with get_kmer_value that
   reads MSB-first, matching debruijn's Kmer8::to_u64() convention.

2. Missing sliding window: every syncmer position was recorded as a
   superkmer boundary. Implemented MSP's sliding window algorithm with
   MinPos struct (rightmost-position tiebreaking) that only breaks when
   the best minimizer changes or falls out of the k-mer window.

3. Wrong superkmer generation: complex first/middle/last iterator logic
   with incorrect position adjustments. Replaced with MSP's interval
   logic: size = next_pos + k - 1 - start for non-last, m - start for
   last.

Also:
- Remove InstrumentationData, timing code, and debug eprintln output
- Add profile binary for comparing implementations
- Add .cargo/config.toml with target-cpu=native
- Update deps: rand 0.5->0.9, criterion 0.4->0.5, colored pinned->latest
- Update bench.rs for rand 0.9 API
- Rewrite tests with 11 syncmer tests covering random sequences, tiling
  correctness, edge cases, word boundary crossing, and long sequences
- Update README with fresh benchmark numbers
- Add Cargo.lock to .gitignore (library crate)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
